zotero.abstractNote: Probabilistic numerical methods for ordinary differential equations (ODEs) quantify the uncertainty arising from the discretization of the continuous-time problem. These methods typically rely on a specific information operator, such as function evaluations or Taylor coefficients, to update the belief about the solution. In this paper, we propose a framework that allows for the flexible combination of different information operators within a single probabilistic ODE solver. We show that this 'pick-and-mix' approach allows for the construction of solvers that are more efficient and robust than those relying on a single type of information. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on a range of numerical experiments.
zotero.conferenceName: 25th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Statistics

extra.Editors: Camps-Valls, Gustau and Ruiz, Francisco J. R. and Valera, Isabel

zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 25th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Statistics
